text:
🇹🇭 THAILAND — TAX STRUCTURE (NON-RESIDENT FOCUS)
1. Why this matters
Defines tax exposure on rental income, property disposal, ownership costs, and transaction-related taxes for non-resident investors.
2. Scope Note
(Official Context)
This note applies to non-resident individual ownership of real estate and does not cover corporate/entity structures.
3. Taxes Imposed
Rental Income Tax
(Official Fact)
Rental income paid to non-residents is subject to:
15% withholding tax
4. Property Disposal Taxes (No Separate CGT)
(Official Fact)
Thailand does not impose a separate capital gains tax on property.
(Official Fact)
Instead, property disposal is subject to multiple taxes:
Withholding Tax
Calculated based on appraised value or declared sale price
Treated as advance payment of income tax
Personal Income Tax (PIT)
Applies based on calculated gain
Uses progressive tax rates
Calculation method depends on holding period
Specific Business Tax (SBT)
Typically 3.3% (including local tax)
Applies if property held for less than 5 years
Stamp Duty
0.5% of transaction value
Applies in cases where SBT is not applicable
5. Property-Related Ongoing Costs
(Official Fact)
Land and Building Tax
Annual tax based on property use and value
Applies to land and buildings
6. Taxes Not Imposed
(Official Fact)
Thailand does not have a separate capital gains tax regime.
7. Estate / Inheritance Tax
(Official Fact)
Inheritance tax applies:
Up to 10% on value exceeding statutory thresholds
8. Advisory Caution
(Advisory Caution)
Tax exposure depends on:
Holding period (affects SBT applicability)
Ownership structure
Transaction value
(Advisory Caution)
Multiple taxes apply at disposal, making total tax burden transaction-specific.
9. What Needs Legal Confirmation (Live Case)
Applicability of Specific Business Tax
Calculation of withholding tax and PIT
Land and building tax obligations
Exact transaction tax breakdown
10. Sources
https://www.rd.go.th
https://www.dol.go.th
11. Verification Date
07/04/2026
12. Escalation Note
ESCALATION REQUIRED –
Property disposal tax treatment involves multiple components (withholding tax, PIT, SBT) and must be calculated on a transaction-specific basis.
